The Alstom Metropolis is a series of metro EMUs in service across the world since 1993. This is a fully automated variant for the Sydney Metro that entered service in 2019. They are expandable from 6 to 8 cars.
The K sets are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by A Goninan & Co that entered service in 1981. Cost and acceleration are guesstimates.
The T sets / Tangaras are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by A Goninan & Co that entered service in 1988.

The A and B sets / Waratahs are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by Downer Rail and CRRC that entered service in 2011.
The D sets / Mariyung are double-decker commuter rail EMUs built for Sydney Trains by Hyundai Rotem, UGC, and Mitsubishi that entered service in 2024.
The Series 2070 is a commuter rail EMU built by Alstom, Bombardier, and CAF for Sao Paulo that entered service in 2008.

The 8000-series is a subway EMU built by Hitachi for Washington DC's Subway that will enter service in 2028. Acceleration is a guesstimate.
The CQ 400 is a subway EMU built by Stadler for the Atlanta metro which will enter service in 2026.
The CQ 312 is a subway EMU built by Stadler for the Atlanta metro which entered service in 2001. Cost is an estimate.

The Siemens Desiro Classic is a family of metro DMUs and EMUs in service across the world since 1999. This model is the EMU used by Athens commuter rail. Cost is an estimate.
The Istanbul Metro has a variety of nameless rolling stock. This is the heavy metro stock that entered service in 2010 on the M4, as made by CAF. Acceleration is a guesstimate.
